title: Disconnect between Chinese iron ore imports and steel production widens
section_path: Disconnect between Chinese iron ore imports and steel production widens
section_path_text: Disconnect between Chinese iron ore imports and steel production widens
summary:
Main
Chinese iron ore imports continue to grow even as domestic steel output weakens, driving port inventories toward record highs. Until Simandou's accelerating ramp-up is offset by cuts elsewhere in the supply chain, the inventory overhang is set to deepen, complicating the demand outlook for traditional exporters. Image reference: unnamed.png | assets/2026-06-05_disconnect-between-chinese-iron-ore-imports-and-steel-production-widens_img_unnamed_d1042380c748.png
Key Takeaways
● Global iron ore flows in May 2026 were down 2% y/y.

children.title: Main

Until Simandou's accelerating ramp-up is offset by cuts elsewhere in the supply chain, the inventory overhang is set to deepen, complicating the demand outlook for traditional exporters.

children.title: Guinea to China to boost cape rates even if total volumes fall
section_path: Guinea to China to boost cape rates even if total volumes fall
children.section_path_text: Guinea to China to boost cape rates even if total volumes fall
children.summary: Iron ore flows from Guinea reached over 2 million tons in May, a record and almost double the previous record from the previous month. Despite this, Guinea still only accounted for 2% of all global iron ore flows in May 2026.
